input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use a **naming convention** condition in `Directory.Build.props`, since it's evaluated *before* package references (so `IsTestProject` isn't available yet):

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- Properties for ALL projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Properties for test projects only -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why not `IsTestProject`?** — `Directory.Build.props` is evaluated before NuGet package imports, so `IsTestProject` (set by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`) isn't `true` yet.

**Alternative:** If your test projects don't follow a single naming pattern, use `Directory.Build.targets` instead — it's evaluated *after* packages, so `IsTestProject` works:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.targets -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Pick whichever fits your repo — naming convention → `.props`, package-based detection → `.targets`.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conditionals. I can answer this directly.

The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks whether the project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` or has `IsTestProject` set to true. MSBuild 17+ sets `IsTestProject` automatically when `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` is referenced, but since `Directory.Build.props` is evaluated before package references, you typically need to either:


1. Use a naming convention (e.g., `$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))`)
2. Set `IsTestProject` manually in test projects and condition on it in `Directory.Build.props`
3. Use `Directory.Build.targets` instead (evaluated after package references, so `IsTestProject` is available)

The cleanest approach is either a naming convention or using `Directory.Build.targets` where the `IsTestProject` property is already available after package references are processed.
